macro_segments.title: The Kohen Gadol's Preparation and the Cow Offering: Shechita and Blood Management

micro_segments.title: Slaughtering the cow and receiving the blood
Stirring the blood to prevent congealing and its location on the fourth step
Preparing the fire pan and clearing coals from the outer altar

macro_segments.title: The Special Fire and Incense Vessels for Yom Kippur: Contrasting Daily Practice with Today's Ritual

micro_segments.title: The separate fire pile created specifically for today's coals
Differences in fire pans: silver vs. gold, and single vs. double transfer on normal days vs. today
Measurements of coal quantities: four kavin vs. three kavin, with Rabbi Yose's variant
Weight and handle of the fire pan: lighter and longer on today vs. heavy on normal days
Color and material of fire pans: greenish-yellow vs. red gold on today
Daily incense quantity and fineness: half maneh vs. double handful, finest on today
Kohen movements around the altar: east and west sides on normal days, center on today
Hand and foot washing locations: communal kiyor vs. special gold vessel on today
Number of fire piles: four normally vs. five today, with Rabbi Yose's and Rabbi Yehuda's variants

macro_segments.title: The Prohibition of Human Presence During Kapparah: Exodus 30:10 and Its Application

micro_segments.title: The problem of the blood-stirrer sitting on the steps while the Kohen Gadol enters
Deriving that nobody can be in the Ohel Moed when atonement occurs
Extending the prohibition beyond incense to blood offerings of special sacrifices

macro_segments.title: The Role of Ketores in Kapparah and Daily Separation Practice

micro_segments.title: Challenging the assumption that ketores effects atonement
The ketores as atonement for lashon hara, derived from the Korach narrative

macro_segments.title: Separation from the Outer Space: Between the Ulam and Mizbeach During Incense Burning

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah's teaching that people must separate from the space between Ulam and Mizbeach during ketores
Rabbi Elazar's reorientation: only daily ketores on the gold altar requires separation from this space, not Yom Kippur ketores
Baraita of Rabbi Yose: separation also when blood of special sacrifices is brought

macro_segments.title: Holiness Gradations and the Coherence of Inner Sanctum Prohibitions

micro_segments.title: The question: what higher status does the Heichal have over the space between Ulam and Mizbeach?
Distinguishing two contexts: separation from Heichal during all kapparah acts vs. separation from outer space only during ketores
Gemara's challenge: If Rabbi Elazar's reading is correct, why doesn't the Baraita mention the difference about Yom Kippur ketores?
Resolution: the Baraita implicitly contrasts daily ketores burning with Yom Kippur inner-sanctum burning
Rabbi Rava's argument that the Ulam and Heichal must share one level of holiness
Final answer: Ulam-Heichal is one conceptual space, distinct from between Ulam and Mizbeach, both mandated by Torah
